learning.summary: Cloud-based and browser-based applications run through a web browser instead of requiring an operating-system-specific executable. This approach allows many applications to work across different operating systems while often relying on network connectivity.
learning.explanation:
Browser-based applications are accessed through a web browser rather than being installed as traditional desktop software. Because the browser provides a common platform, many web applications can run on Windows, macOS, Linux, and Chrome OS without separate versions for each operating system.

Many browser-based applications are also cloud-based, meaning application logic, storage, or services are hosted on remote systems. As a result, an internet or network connection is often required for full functionality.

Some web applications provide limited offline capabilities, but many features depend on connectivity to synchronize data or access cloud services. Web applications reduce many operating-system compatibility concerns because the browser acts as the common runtime environment.

facts.text: Browser-based applications run inside a web browser.
Many web applications work across multiple operating systems.
Cloud-based applications commonly require network connectivity.
Some web applications have limited functionality when offline.

examples.text: A user signs in to an online office suite from Windows, macOS, or Linux using a supported web browser.

examTips.text: Differentiate browser-based applications from OS-specific executable applications.

commonMistakes.text: Assuming every web application functions fully without an internet connection.

scenarios.situation: Recommend a cross-platform application solution for users with different operating systems.
scenarios.expectedAction: Identify the correct concept or response.

pbqIdeas.task: Classify applications as traditional desktop software or browser-based applications.

reviewNotes: Merged network connectivity guidance into this object per discovery review.
Offline capability statement is generalized enrichment beyond the directly cited evidence.
